title: Canadian Securities Litigation Trends: Today’s Regulatory Environment and Risk Mitigation Strategies for Directors & Officers
content:
## Canadian Securities Litigation Trends: Today’s Regulatory Environment and Risk Mitigation Strategies for Directors & Officers
Capital market participants in Canada face a minefield of emerging securities regulatory and litigation risks, from the rising wave of climate disclosure-related litigation and shareholder activism to the enhanced scrutiny of cybersecurity controls. In this evolving and constantly changing environment, it is more important than ever for Directors and Officers to stay abreast of these developments and ensure they protect themselves and their organizations from these risks.
Join us in person for a panel discussion hosted by McCarthy Tétrault’s Securities Litigation Group and HUB International’s Executive Risk Practice (ProEx) to better understand these developments and the possible risks that organizations, Directors and Officers face.
Following this interesting discussion, attendees will:
- Obtain an overview of key trends and developments in Canadian securities regulatory enforcement.
- Learn about the rapidly evolving landscape for sustainability-related disclosures and the evolving litigation risk environment.
- Hear about recent trends and developments regarding cybersecurity related securities class actions and regulatory enforcement.
- Engage in discussions on the duties of directors and officers, risk mitigation strategies, and insurance considerations.
- Reid Irwin , Senior Vice President, Practice Leader for Executive Risk & Financial Institutions, HUB International
- Wendy Berman , Partner and Head of Securities Litigation, McCarthy Tétrault LLP
- Julie-Martine Loranger , Partner, Litigation Group, McCarthy Tétrault LLP
- Jonathan Nehmetallah , Associate, Litigation Group, McCarthy Tétrault LLP
A reception will follow the presentation. For questions about this event, please contact clientevents@mccarthy.ca .
This program qualifies for up to 1 hour of CPD credit under the mandatory education regimes in British Columbia and Ontario.
Barreau du Québec Continuing Education: This program contains up to 1 hour of content. For more information regarding the declaration and recognition of educational activities, please contact the Barreau du Québec .
Time: 5:00 - 7:00 p.m. (ET)
- Jonathan Nehmetallah Partner People.Offices.Singular Toronto
